content.problemStatement:
**This is the hard version of the problem. In this version, the labels of the nodes AiA_iAi​ satisfy Ai≤NA_i \le NAi​≤N.

This problem is worth 656565 points in division 111, and is not scorable in the other divisions.**

You are given a labeled tree on NNN nodes. Node iii has the label AiA_iAi​.

Count the number of zig-zag sequences in it.

Formally, a sequence of nodes v1,v2,v3,…,vkv_1, v_2, v_3, \ldots, v_kv1​,v2​,v3​,…,vk​ of length k≥2k \ge 2k≥2 is a **zig-zag sequence** if the concatenation of simple paths from v1v_1v1​ to v2v_2v2​, from v2v_2v2​ to v3v_3v3​, ..., from vk−1v_{k-1}vk−1​ to vkv_kvk​ forms a simple path, and either Av1<Av2>Av3<…A_{v_1} < A_{v_2} > A_{v_3} < \ldotsAv1​​<Av2​​>Av3​​<… or Av1>Av2<Av3>…A_{v_1} > A_{v_2} < A_{v_3} > \ldotsAv1​​>Av2​​<Av3​​>…, where AvA_vAv​ is the label of node vvv.

222 zig-zag sequences are different if either their lengths are different, or there exists at least 111 node which is included in the first sequence but not in the second sequence. (1,2,3)(1, 2, 3)(1,2,3) and (3,2,1)(3, 2, 1)(3,2,1) are considered to be the same sequence.

The answer can be large, so print it modulo 109+710^9 + 7109+7.

A tree is a connected acyclic graph. A path is simple if it traverses each edge at most once.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of three lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains one integer NNN, the number of nodes in the tree.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,…,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​, where AiA_iAi​ is the label of node iii.
The third line contains (N−1)(N - 1)(N−1) space-separated integers P2,P3,…,PNP_2, P_3, \ldots, P_NP2​,P3​,…,PN​, denoting an edge between iii and PiP_iPi​.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line the number of different zig-zag sequences, modulo 109+710^9 + 7109+7.
constraints: The sum of NNN does not exceed 10510^5105 over all test cases.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: The following are the valid zig-zag sequences in the tree.

(1, 2)
(1, 3)
(1, 4)
(1, 5)
(2, 1, 3)
(2, 1, 4)
(2, 1, 5)
(2, 3)
(2, 4)
(2, 5)
(3, 1, 4)
(3, 1, 5)
(3, 4)
(4, 1, 5)
(4, 5)
